cards.grammarNote: V/A stem + 지만 = but / however. 피곤하다 (to be tired) → 피곤하지만. Works with any verb or adjective. The subject is the same in both clauses.
어렵다 (to be difficult) → 어렵지만 (no stem change needed). 재미있다 = to be fun/interesting. Common pattern for expressing mixed feelings.
무섭다 (to be scary/scared) → 무섭지만. 설레다 = to be excited/flutter with anticipation. Great emotional contrast.

cards.front: I like him but we can't date.
cards.grammarNote: 좋아하다 (to like) → 좋아하지만. 사귀다 = to date. 사귈 수 없어요 = cannot date.
슬프다 (to be sad) → 슬프지만. 괜찮다 = to be okay/fine. A common phrase for expressing resilience.
화가 나다 (to be angry/anger rises) → 화가 나지만. 이해하다 = to understand.

cards.front: I miss him but I've moved on.
cards.grammarNote: 그리워하다 (to miss someone) → 그리워하지만. 이제 = now. Expression of emotional progress.

cards.front: I'm lonely but I feel free.
cards.grammarNote: 외롭다 (to be lonely) → 외롭지만 (ㅂ irregular doesn't apply with 지만; stem stays as is before consonants). 자유롭다 = to be free.

cards.front: I'm nervous but I can do it.
cards.grammarNote: 긴장되다 (to feel nervous) → 긴장되지만. 할 수 있어요 = can do it. An encouraging contrast.

cards.front: The job is boring but the pay is good.
cards.grammarNote: 지루하다 (to be boring) → 지루하지만. 월급 = monthly salary/pay. -지만 can also contrast subjects across clauses.

cards.front: I'm jealous but I'm happy for you.
cards.grammarNote: 부럽다 (to be envious/jealous) → 부럽지만. 잘됐다 = to have turned out well / good for you. A gracious emotional response.
